3	import logging
4	import time
5	from concurrent import futures
6	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, List
7	
8	import torch
9	
10	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
11	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
12	    create_grammar_backend,
13	)
14	from sglang.srt.environ import envs
15	
16	if TYPE_CHECKING:
17	    from sglang.srt.managers.io_struct import AbortReq
18	    from sglang.srt.managers.schedule_batch import Req
19	    from sglang.srt.managers.scheduler import Scheduler
20	
21	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
22	
23	
24	class GrammarManager:
25	    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
26	        self.scheduler = scheduler
27	        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
28	        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
29	        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
30	            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
31	                self.server_args,
32	                scheduler.tokenizer,
33	                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
34	                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
35	            )
36	        else:
37	            self.grammar_backend = None
38	
39	        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
40	        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
41	        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
42	        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank
43	
44	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
45	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
46	            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
47	        )
48	
49	    def __len__(self):
50	        return len(self.grammar_queue)
51	
52	    def clear(self):
53	        if self.grammar_backend:
54	            self.grammar_backend.reset()
55	
56	    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
57	        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0
58	
59	    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
60	        for req in self.grammar_queue:
61	            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
62	                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
63	                if req.grammar:
64	                    req.grammar.cancel()
65	                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")
66	
67	    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
68	        # Init grammar cache for this request
69	        add_to_grammar_queue = False
70	        if (
71	            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
72	            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
73	            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
74	            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
75	        ):
76	            if self.grammar_backend is None:
77	                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
78	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
79	            else:
80	                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
81	                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
82	                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
83	                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
84	                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
85	                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
86	                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
87	                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
88	
89	                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
90	                    key, req.require_reasoning
91	                )
92	                req.grammar = value
93	
94	                if not cache_hit:
95	                    req.grammar_key = key
96	                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
97	                else:
98	                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
99	                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
100	                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
101	
102	        if add_to_grammar_queue:
103	            self.grammar_queue.append(req)
104	
105	        return add_to_grammar_queue
106	
107	    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
108	        """
109	        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.
110	
111	        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
112	        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
113	        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)
114	
115	        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
116	        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
117	        """
118	        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
119	        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
120	
121	        # Poll for ready requests
122	        start_time = time.perf_counter()
123	        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
124	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
125	                if i in ready_req_idxs:
126	                    continue
127	
128	                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
129	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
130	                    continue
131	
132	                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
133	                if req.grammar.done():
134	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
135	
136	            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
137	            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)
138	
139	        # Check failed requests
140	        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
141	            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
142	                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
143	                if (
144	                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
145	                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
146	                ):
147	                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
148	                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
149	                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)
150	
151	        # Sync ready and failed requests across all ranks
152	        if self.grammar_sync_size == 1:
153	            synced_ready_req_idxs = ready_req_idxs
154	            synced_failed_req_idxs = failed_req_idxs
155	        else:
156	            all_gather_output = [None] * self.grammar_sync_size
157	            torch.distributed.all_gather_object(
158	                all_gather_output,
159	                (ready_req_idxs, failed_req_idxs),
160	                group=self.grammar_sync_group,
161	            )
162	            synced_ready_req_idxs = set.intersection(*[x[0] for x in all_gather_output])
163	            synced_failed_req_idxs = set.union(*[x[1] for x in all_gather_output])
164	
165	        # Return ready requests
166	        return_reqs: List[Req] = []
167	        for i in synced_ready_req_idxs:
168	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
169	            return_reqs.append(req)
170	            if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
171	                continue
172	
173	            req.grammar = req.grammar.result()
174	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, req.grammar.copy())
175	            if req.grammar is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
176	                error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request: {req.grammar_key=}"
177	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
178	
179	        # Return failed requests
180	        for i in synced_failed_req_idxs:
181	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
182	            return_reqs.append(req)
183	
184	            req.grammar.cancel()
185	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ)
186	            error_msg = f"Grammar preprocessing timed out: {req.grammar_key=}"
187	            req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
188	
189	        # Remove finished requests from grammar_queue
190	        self.grammar_queue = [
191	            req
192	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue)
193	            if i not in synced_ready_req_idxs and i not in synced_failed_req_idxs
194	        ]
195	        return return_reqs

392	    def end(self):
393	        if self.use_thread:
394	            if self.worker.is_alive():
395	                self.queue.put(None)

398	    def _thread_worker_func(self):
399	        error = None
400	
401	        while True:
402	            expr = self.queue.get()
403	            if expr is None:
404	                self.queue.task_done()
405	                break
406	
407	            try:
408	                self._execute(expr)
409	            except Exception as e:
410	                warnings.warn(f"Error in stream_executor: {get_exception_traceback()}")
411	                error = e
412	                break
413	            self.queue.task_done()
414	            if self.stream_text_event:
415	                self.stream_text_event.set()
416	
417	        # Clean the queue and events
418	        if error is not None:
419	            try:
420	                while True:
421	                    self.queue.task_done()
422	                    self.queue.get_nowait()
423	            except queue.Empty:
424	                pass
425	            for name in self.variable_event:
426	                self.variable_event[name].set()
427	            if self.stream_var_event:
428	                for name in self.stream_var_event:
429	                    self.stream_var_event[name].set()
430	            self.error_ = error
431	
432	        if self.stream_text_event:
433	            self.stream_text_event.set()
434	
435	        self.is_finished = True

437	    def _execute(self, other):
438	        if isinstance(other, str):

443	        if isinstance(other, SglConstantText):

445	        elif isinstance(other, SglGen):
446	            self._execute_gen(other)
447	        elif isinstance(other, SglSelect):
448	            self._execute_select(other)
449	        elif isinstance(other, SglExprList):
450	            for x in other.expr_list:
451	                self._execute(x)
452	        elif isinstance(other, SglRoleBegin):
453	            self._execute_role_begin(other)
454	        elif isinstance(other, SglRoleEnd):
455	            self._execute_role_end(other)
456	        elif isinstance(other, SglImage):
457	            self._execute_image(other)
458	        elif isinstance(other, SglVideo):
459	            self._execute_video(other)
460	        elif isinstance(other, SglVariable):
461	            self._execute_variable(other)
462	        elif isinstance(other, SglVarScopeBegin):
463	            self._execute_var_scope_begin(other)
464	        elif isinstance(other, SglVarScopeEnd):

42	def run_internal(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync):
43	    try:
44	        state.ret_value = program.func(state, *func_args, **func_kwargs)
45	    except Exception as e:
46	        raise e
47	    finally:
48	        state.stream_executor.end()
49	
50	    if sync:
51	        state.stream_executor.sync()
52	
53	    if global_config.verbosity >= 2:
54	        print(state.text())

57	def run_program(
58	    program,
59	    backend,
60	    func_args,
61	    func_kwargs,
62	    default_sampling_para,
63	    stream,
64	    sync=False,
65	    use_thread=True,
66	):
67	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
68	        backend = backend.endpoint
69	    assert backend is not None, "Please specify a backend"
70	    func_kwargs.update(program.bind_arguments)
71	    stream_executor = StreamExecutor(
72	        backend,
73	        func_kwargs,
74	        default_sampling_para,
75	        chat_template=None,
76	        stream=stream,
77	        num_api_spec_tokens=program.num_api_spec_tokens,
78	        use_thread=use_thread,
79	    )
80	    state = ProgramState(stream_executor)
81	
82	    if stream:
83	        t = threading.Thread(
84	            target=run_internal, args=(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync)
85	        )
86	        t.start()
87	        return state
88	    else:
89	        run_internal(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync)
90	        return state

93	def run_program_batch(
94	    program,
95	    backend,
96	    batch_arguments,
97	    default_sampling_para,
98	    num_threads,
99	    progress_bar,
100	    generator_style=False,
101	):
102	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
103	        backend = backend.endpoint
104	
105	    # Pre-cache the common prefix for a batch. The prefix is extracted by tracing the program.
106	    if global_config.enable_precache_with_tracing and len(batch_arguments) > 1:
107	        cache_program(program, backend)
108	
109	    # Run all programs
110	    if num_threads == "auto":
111	        num_threads = max(96, multiprocessing.cpu_count() * 16)
112	    num_threads = min(num_threads, len(batch_arguments))
113	
114	    if generator_style:
115	        return _run_program_batch_generator(
116	            program,
117	            backend,
118	            batch_arguments,
119	            default_sampling_para,
120	            num_threads,
121	            progress_bar,
122	        )
123	
124	    # Original code path when generator_style=False
125	    if num_threads == 1:
126	        rets = []
127	        if progress_bar:
128	            for arguments in tqdm.tqdm(batch_arguments):
129	                rets.append(
130	                    run_program(
131	                        program,
132	                        backend,
133	                        (),
134	                        arguments,
135	                        default_sampling_para,
136	                        False,
137	                        True,
138	                    )
139	                )
140	        else:
141	            for arguments in batch_arguments:
142	                rets.append(
143	                    run_program(
144	                        program,
145	                        backend,
146	                        (),
147	                        arguments,
148	                        default_sampling_para,
149	                        False,
150	                        True,
151	                    )
152	                )
153	    else:
154	        if progress_bar:
155	            pbar = tqdm.tqdm(total=len(batch_arguments))
156	
157	        with ThreadPoolExecutor(num_threads) as executor:
158	            futures = []
159	            for arguments in batch_arguments:
160	                futures.append(
161	                    executor.submit(
162	                        run_program,
163	                        program,
164	                        backend,
165	                        (),
166	                        arguments,
167	                        default_sampling_para,
168	                        False,
169	                        True,
170	                    )
171	                )
172	                if progress_bar:
173	                    futures[-1].add_done_callback(lambda _: pbar.update())
174	
175	            rets = [f.result() for f in futures]
176	        rets[-1].sync()
177	
178	        if progress_bar:
179	            pbar.close()
180	
181	    return rets

714	    def _execute_concatenate_and_append_kv_cache(self, expr: SglConcateAndAppend):
715	        self_len = len(self.text_)
716	
717	        for i, s in enumerate(expr.states):
718	            exe = s.stream_executor
719	            exe.submit(SglCommitLazy())
720	
721	        for i, s in enumerate(expr.states):
722	            exe = s.stream_executor

725	            self.text_ += exe.text_[exe.fork_start_text_pos :]

727	        src_rids = [state.stream_executor.sid for state in expr.states]

775	    def _resolve_sampling_params(self, sampling_params):
776	        """
777	        Construct sampling param based on default + override values
778	
779	        The default values of sampling are populated in `default_sampling_para` via sgl.function.run(...sampling_args)
780	        , and `sampling_params` contains the override values from sgl.gen().
781	
782	        Here we use default_sampling_para as the base and override the values if they exist in `sampling_params`.
783	        It also extends the stop tokens based on the chat template.
784	        """
785	
786	        # deepcopy is required because the dict has lists inside
787	        clone = copy.deepcopy(self.default_sampling_para)
788	
789	        for item in [
790	            "max_new_tokens",
791	            "min_new_tokens",
792	            "n",
793	            "stop",
794	            "stop_token_ids",
795	            "stop_regex",
796	            "temperature",
797	            "top_p",
798	            "top_k",
799	            "min_p",
800	            "frequency_penalty",
801	            "presence_penalty",
802	            "ignore_eos",
803	            "return_logprob",
804	            "logprob_start_len",
805	            "top_logprobs_num",
806	            "return_text_in_logprobs",
807	            "dtype",
808	            "regex",
809	            "json_schema",
810	        ]:
811	            value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)
812	            if value is not None:
813	                setattr(clone, item, value)
814	
815	        if self.chat_template.stop_str:
816	            if clone.stop == ():
817	                clone.stop = []
818	            elif isinstance(clone.stop, str):
819	                clone.stop = [clone.stop]
820	            clone.stop += self.chat_template.stop_str
821	
822	        return clone

55 56 ## About 57 SGLang is a high-performance serving framework for large language models and multimodal models. 58 It is designed to deliver low-latency and high-throughput inference across a wide range of setups, from a single GPU to large distributed clusters. 59 Its core features include: 60 61 - **Fast Runtime**: Provides efficient serving with RadixAttention for prefix caching, a zero-overhead CPU scheduler, prefill-decode disaggregation, speculative decoding, continuous batching, paged attention, tensor/pipeline/expert/data parallelism, structured outputs, chunked prefill, quantization (FP4/FP8/INT4/AWQ/GPTQ), and multi-LoRA batching. 62 - **Broad Model Support**: Supports a wide range of language models (Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek, Kimi, GLM, GPT, Gemma, Mistral, etc.), embedding models (e5-mistral, gte, mcdse), reward models (Skywork), and diffusion models (WAN, Qwen-Image), with easy extensibility for adding new models. Compatible with most Hugging Face models and OpenAI APIs. 63 - **Extensive Hardware Support**: Runs on NVIDIA GPUs (GB200/B300/H100/A100/Spark), AMD GPUs (MI355/MI300), Intel Xeon CPUs, Google TPUs, Ascend NPUs, and more. 64 - **Active Community**: SGLang is open-source and supported by a vibrant community with widespread industry adoption, powering over 400,000 GPUs worldwide. 65 - **RL & Post-Training Backbone**: SGLang is a proven rollout backend across the world, with native RL integrations and adoption by well-known post-training frameworks such as [**AReaL**](https://github.com/inclusionAI/AReaL), [**Miles**](https://github.com/radixark/miles), [**slime**](https://github.com/THUDM/slime), [**Tunix**](https://github.com/google/tunix), [**verl**](https://github.com/volcengine/verl) and more. 66 67 ## Getting Started 68 - [Install SGLang](https://docs.sglang.io/get_started/install.html) 69 - [Quick Start](https://docs.sglang.io/basic_usage/send_request.html) 70 - [Backend Tutorial](https://docs.sglang.io/basic_usage/openai_api_completions.html) 71 - [Frontend Tutorial](https://docs.sglang.io/references/frontend/frontend_tutorial.html) 72 - [Contribution Guide](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html) 73 74 ## Benchmark and Performance 75 Learn more in the release blogs: [v0.2 blog](https://lmsys.org/blog/2024-07-25-sglang-llama3/), [v0.3 blog](https://lmsys.org/blog/2024-09-04-sglang-v0-3/), [v0.4 blog](https://lmsys.org/blog/2024-12-04-sglang-v0-4/), [Large-scale expert parallelism](https://lmsys.org/blog/2025-05-05-large-scale-ep/), [GB200 rack-scale parallelism](https://lmsys.org/blog/2025-09-25-gb200-part-2/). 76 77 ## Adoption and Sponsorship 78 SGLang has been deployed at large scale, generating trillions of tokens in production each day. It is trusted and adopted by a wide range of leading enterprises and institutions, including xAI, AMD, NVIDIA, Intel, LinkedIn, Cursor, Oracle Cloud, Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure, AWS, Atlas Cloud, Voltage Park, Nebius, DataCrunch, Novita, InnoMatrix, MIT, UCLA, the University of Washington, Stanford, UC Berkeley, Tsinghua University, Jam & Tea Studios, Baseten, and other major technology organizations across North America and Asia. 79 As an open-source LLM inference engine, SGLang has become the de facto industry standard, with deployments running on over 400,000 GPUs worldwide. 80 SGLang is currently hosted under the non-profit open-source organization [LMSYS](https://lmsys.org/about/).

1	import argparse
2	import json
3	import time
4	from typing import List, Tuple
5	
6	import jsonschema
7	from datasets import load_dataset
8	
9	import sglang as sgl
10	from sglang.global_config import global_config
11	from sglang.srt.utils.hf_transformers_utils import get_tokenizer
12	from sglang.test.test_utils import (
13	    add_common_sglang_args_and_parse,
14	    select_sglang_backend,
15	)
16	from sglang.utils import dump_state_text
17	
18	
19	@sgl.function
20	def schema_gen(s, message: Tuple[str, str], json_schema: str):
21	    system, user = message
22	    s += sgl.system(system)
23	    s += sgl.user(user)
24	    s += sgl.assistant(
25	        sgl.gen("json_output", temperature=0, max_tokens=256, json_schema=json_schema)
26	    )
27	
28	
29	def contains_formats(schema, formats: List[str]):
30	    if isinstance(schema, dict):
31	        if schema.get("format", None) in formats:
32	            return True
33	        for value in schema.values():
34	            if contains_formats(value, formats):
35	                return True
36	    elif isinstance(schema, list):
37	        for item in schema:
38	            if contains_formats(item, formats):
39	                return True
40	    return False
41	
42	
43	def convert_dataset(path: str):
44	    raw_dataset = load_dataset(path)
45	    dataset = []
46	    for data in raw_dataset["train"]:
47	        messages = data["prompt"]
48	        schema = data["schema"]
49	        obj = json.loads(schema)
50	
51	        # skip some corrupted examples
52	        if obj.get("type", None) is None:
53	            continue
54	
55	        # skip schema with format "email"
56	        # which is not supported by outlines for now
57	        if contains_formats(obj, ["email"]):
58	            continue
59	
60	        system = messages[0]
61	        user = messages[1]
62	        assert system["role"] == "system", "invalid role"
63	        assert user["role"] == "user", "invalid role"
64	        assert len(messages) == 2, "invalid message length"
65	        message = json.dumps(system["content"]), json.dumps(user["content"])
66	        dataset.append(
67	            {
68	                "message": message,
69	                "json_schema": schema,
70	            }
71	        )
72	
73	    return dataset
74	
75	
76	def bench_schema(args):
77	    arguments = convert_dataset(args.data_path)
78	
79	    if args.num_jsons < 0 or args.num_jsons > len(arguments):
80	        args.num_jsons = len(arguments)
81	    arguments = arguments[: args.num_jsons]
82	
83	    # Select backend
84	    backend = select_sglang_backend(args)
85	    sgl.set_default_backend(backend)
86	
87	    # Run requests
88	    tic = time.perf_counter()
89	    states = schema_gen.run_batch(
90	        arguments,
91	        temperature=0,
92	        num_threads=args.parallel,
93	        progress_bar=True,
94	    )
95	    latency = time.perf_counter() - tic
96	
97	    # Check if the outputs are valid
98	    indexes = []
99	    for i, state in enumerate(states):
100	        try:
101	            schema = json.loads(arguments[i]["json_schema"])
102	            obj = json.loads(state["json_output"])
103	            assert jsonschema.validate(obj, schema) is None
104	        except Exception as e:
105	            print(e)
106	            indexes.append(i)
107	
108	    return states, latency
109	
110	
111	def main(args):
112	    states, latency = bench_schema(args)
113	
114	    # Compute accuracy
115	    tokenizer = get_tokenizer(
116	        global_config.default_backend.get_server_info()["tokenizer_path"]
117	    )
118	    output_jsons = [state["json_output"] for state in states]
119	    num_output_tokens = sum(len(tokenizer.encode(x)) for x in output_jsons)
120	    print(f"Latency: {latency:.3f}")
121	    print(f"Output throughput: {num_output_tokens / latency:.3f} token/s")
122	    print(f"#output tokens: {num_output_tokens}")
123	
124	    # Write results
125	    dump_state_text(f"tmp_output_{args.backend}.txt", states)
126	    with open(f"{args.backend}.jsonl", "w") as fout:
127	        for state in states:
128	            fout.write(state["json_output"] + "\n")
129	
130	    with open(args.result_file, "a") as fout:
131	        value = {
132	            "task": "json_schema",
133	            "backend": args.backend,
134	            "latency": round(latency, 3),
135	            "num_jsons": args.num_jsons,
136	            "parallel": args.parallel,
137	        }
138	        fout.write(json.dumps(value) + "\n")
139	
140	
141	if __name__ == "__main__":
142	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
143	    parser.add_argument("--data-path", type=str, default="NousResearch/json-mode-eval")
144	    parser.add_argument("--num-jsons", type=int, default=-1)
145	    args = add_common_sglang_args_and_parse(parser)
146	    main(args)

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me prepare the final report:

## SGLang Codebase Structure Report

### 1. Top-Level Directory Structure

The repository contains:
- **python/** - Main Python source code directory
- **sgl-kernel/** - Low-level kernel implementation
- **sgl-model-gateway/** - Model gateway service
- **docker/** - Docker configurations
- **docs/** - Documentation
- **examples/** - Example code
- **benchmark/** - Benchmarking scripts
- **test/** - Test suite
- **scripts/** - Utility scripts
- **3rdparty/** - Third-party dependencies

### 2. Core Module Organization under `python/sglang/`

The main Python package is organized into three key subsystems:

#### A. **Frontend Language Layer** (`sglang/lang/`)
- **api.py** - Public APIs for the SGLang programming language
  - `gen()` - Generate text with constraints (regex, json_schema, dtype)
  - `gen_int()` / `gen_string()` - Type-specific generation
  - `select()` - Choose between discrete options
  - `function()` - Define SGL programs
  - Role/chat functions: `system()`, `user()`, `assistant()`

- **ir.py** - Intermediate Representation (IR) classes
  - `SglGen` - Generation node with sampling parameters
  - `SglSamplingParams` - Sampling configuration including constraints:
    - `dtype` - Target data type (int, str, bool, float)
    - `regex` - Regex pattern constraint
    - `json_schema` - JSON schema constraint
    - `ebnf` - Extended Backus-Naur Form constraint
  - `SglSelect` - Selection/choice node
  - `SglFunction` - Decorated SGL programs
  - Various expression types: `SglConstantText`, `SglRoleBegin/End`, etc.

- **interpreter.py** - Executes SGL programs
  - `run_program()` - Single program execution
  - `run_program_batch()` - Batch program execution
  - `StreamExecutor` - Manages streaming execution

- **choices.py** - Choice/selection sampling methods
  - `TokenLengthNormalized` - Normalize scores by token length
  - `GreedyTokenSelection` - Greedy logprob selection
  - `UnconditionalLikelihoodNormalized` - LLM-guided selection

- **chat_template.py** - Chat templating and formatting

- **tracer.py** - Program tracing for optimization

- **backend/** - Backend implementations (OpenAI, Anthropic, LiteLLM, VertexAI, Runtime Endpoint)

#### B. **Runtime/Serving Layer** (`sglang/srt/`)
The high-performance serving engine with extensive subsystems:

**Key Directories:**

- **constrained/** - Constraint/grammar backend implementations
  - **grammar_manager.py** - Main grammar request management
  - **base_grammar_backend.py** - Abstract base class for grammar backends
  - **outlines_backend.py** - Backend using Outlines library
  - **xgrammar_backend.py** - XGrammar backend (recommended for performance)
  - **llguidance_backend.py** - LLGuidance backend
  - **reasoner_grammar_backend.py** - For reasoning models
  - **triton_ops/** - CUDA kernels for vocabulary masking
  - **utils.py** - Helper utilities

- **sampling/** - Sampling and generation control
  - **sampling_params.py** - Sampling parameter classes including constraint options
  - **sampling_batch_info.py** - Batch-level sampling information
  - **custom_logit_processor.py** - Custom logit modification

- **managers/** - Request and resource management
  - **scheduler.py** - Request scheduler
  - **io_struct.py** - Input/output structures

- **function_call/** - Function calling support (27 model-specific detectors)
  - Base format detector, DeepSeek, GLM, Qwen, LLaMA, etc.

- **models/** - Model implementations (168 model directories)
  - Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek, Mistral, Gemma, etc.

- **entrypoints/** - Server entry points and Engine class

- **compilation/** - Dynamic compilation features

- **mem_cache/** - Memory and KV cache management

- **model_executor/** - Model forward pass execution

- **speculative/** - Speculative decoding

- **lora/** - Low-Rank Adaptation support

- **multimodal/** - Multimodal (image/video) processing

### 3. Key Files for Constraints

**Where Constraints are Defined:**

1. **High-level API** (`python/sglang/lang/api.py`):
   - `gen()` accepts `dtype`, `regex`, `json_schema` parameters

2. **IR representation** (`python/sglang/lang/ir.py`):
   - `SglSamplingParams` dataclass with constraint fields
   - Lines 36-40 define constraint-related fields:
     ```python
     json_schema: Optional[str] = None
     dtype: Optional[str] = None
     regex: Optional[str] = None
     ```

3. **Backend sampling** (`python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`):
   - `SamplingParams` class (lines 31-96) includes:
     - `json_schema` - JSON Schema constraint
     - `regex` - Regular expression constraint
     - `ebnf` - EBNF grammar constraint
     - `structural_tag` - Structural tag constraint
   - Also includes `verify()` and `normalize()` methods

4. **Grammar constraint engine** (`python/sglang/srt/constrained/`):
   - Central hub for all constraint processing
   - `GrammarManager` handles constraint compilation and caching
   - Multiple backend implementations (Outlines, XGrammar, LLGuidance)

### 4. Constraint Types Supported

Based on the code analysis:

1. **dtype** - Type constraints (int, str, bool, float, custom types)
   - Implemented via predefined regex patterns in `ir.py`:
     - `REGEX_INT`, `REGEX_FLOAT`, `REGEX_BOOL`, `REGEX_STR`

2. **regex** - Regular expression patterns
   - Processed by grammar backends (Outlines/XGrammar)

3. **json_schema** - JSON Schema constraints
   - Converted to regex FSM by backend
   - Configuration option: `constrained_json_whitespace_pattern`

4. **ebnf** - Extended Backus-Naur Form
   - Supported by some backends (e.g., XGrammar)

5. **structural_tag** - Model-specific structural tags
   - For models like DeepSeek-R1 with reasoning

### 5. Module Organization Summary

```
sglang/
├── lang/                 # User-facing language APIs
│   ├── api.py           # Public API (gen, select, function, etc.)
│   ├── ir.py            # Intermediate representation (SglGen, SglSamplingParams)
│   ├── interpreter.py   # Program execution
│   ├── choices.py       # Choice sampling strategies
│   └── backend/         # Backend connectors
│
└── srt/                 # High-performance runtime
    ├── sampling/        # Sampling parameters and logit processing
    ├── constrained/     # Grammar/constraint backends
    ├── managers/        # Request/resource management
    ├── models/          # Model implementations (168+)
    ├── function_call/   # Function calling
    ├── mem_cache/       # KV cache management
    ├── model_executor/  # Model forward pass
    ├── entrypoints/     # Server entry points
    └── [40+ other subsystems...]
```

### 6. Test and Example Files

**Constraint-related test files:**
- `/python/sglang/test/kits/json_constrained_kit.py` - JSON schema testing
- `/python/sglang/test/kits/regex_constrained_kit.py` - Regex constraint testing

These files contain test mixins for validating JSON and regex-constrained generation.

### 7. Key Configuration

**Grammar backends** (configurable via `--grammar-backend` flag):
- `outlines` - Default, uses Outlines library
- `xgrammar` - High-performance grammar matching
- `llguidance` - Alternative backend
- `none` - Disable constraint support

**Related server args** (`python/sglang/srt/server_args.py`):
- `grammar_backend` - Which backend to use
- `constrained_json_whitespace_pattern` - JSON whitespace handling
- `constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace` - Whitespace options

This structure allows SGLang to support sophisticated constraint-based generation across both the user-facing programming language layer and the high-performance runtime engine.

- `outlines` - Default, uses Outlines library
- `xgrammar` - High-performance grammar matching
- `llguidance` - Alternative backend
- `none` - Disable constraint support

**Related server args** (`python/sglang/srt/server_args.py`):
- `grammar_backend` - Which backend to use
- `constrained_json_whitespace_pattern` - JSON whitespace handling
- `constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace` - Whitespace options

This structure allows SGLang to support sophisticated constraint-based generation across both the user-facing programming language layer and the high-performance runtime engine.

## Summary Report: SGLang's Frontend API for Constraints

Based on my thorough exploration of the SGLang codebase, here's a comprehensive understanding of how users specify and use constraints:

### 1. **Main User-Facing API: `gen()` Function**

The primary function for specifying constraints is **`sgl.gen()`** defined in:
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775392221800-2e0e6a/python/sglang/lang/api.py`

Function signature (lines 75-99):
```python
def gen(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    min_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    n: Optional[int] = None,
    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
    min_p: Optional[float] = None,
    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
    dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]] = None,
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
    regex: Optional[str] = None,              # <-- CONSTRAINT
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,         # <-- CONSTRAINT
):
```

**Constraint parameters:**
- **`regex`** - A Python regex string for constrained generation
- **`json_schema`** - A JSON schema string for structured outputs
- **`dtype`** - Type constraint (int/string/etc.)
- **`choices`** - Set of discrete choices (uses `SglSelect` internally)

### 2. **Frontend Language Usage Patterns**

#### Pattern A: Using the `@sgl.function` decorator
```python
@sgl.function
def character_gen(s, name):
    s += name + " is a character in Harry Potter.\n"
    s += sgl.gen("json_output", max_tokens=256, regex=character_regex)
```

#### Pattern B: With regex constraint (from `/examples/frontend_language/usage/json_decode.py`):
```python
s += sgl.gen("json_output", max_tokens=256, regex=character_regex)
```

#### Pattern C: With JSON schema constraint:
```python
s += sgl.gen("character", max_tokens=128, temperature=0, 
             json_schema=json.dumps(schema_dict))
```

Example from test file (JSON schema):
```python
json_schema = json.dumps({
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
        "name": {"type": "string", "pattern": "^[\\w]+$"},
        "population": {"type": "integer"},
    },
    "required": ["name", "population"],
    "additionalProperties": False,
})
```

#### Pattern D: With Pydantic models
```python
from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import build_regex_from_object

class Wizard(BaseModel):
    name: str
    age: int
    weapon: Weapon

s += sgl.gen("character", max_tokens=128, 
             regex=build_regex_from_object(Wizard))
```

### 3. **Intermediate Representation (IR) Layer**

Constraints are captured in the `SglGen` class (`/python/sglang/lang/ir.py`, line 451):
```python
class SglGen(SglExpr):
    def __init__(self, 
                 # ... other params ...
                 dtype: Optional[type] = None,
                 regex: Optional[str] = None,
                 json_schema: Optional[str] = None):
        self.sampling_params = SglSamplingParams(
            # ... other params ...
            dtype=dtype,
            regex=regex,
            json_schema=json_schema,
        )
```

The `SglSamplingParams` dataclass (lines 18-62 in `ir.py`):
- Stores all constraint-related data
- Provides methods like `to_srt_kwargs()` to pass to the SRT backend
- Validates constraints: only **one** of `json_schema`, `regex`, or `ebnf` can be set

### 4. **Data Flow: Frontend → Backend**

1. **Frontend API** (`sgl.gen()`) → creates `SglGen` IR object
2. **Interpreter** (`/python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py`, `_execute_gen()` method)
3. **StreamExecutor** - executes the gen expression and calls `backend.generate()`
4. **Backend** receives `SamplingParams` with constraints
5. **SRT Engine** uses grammar backend (XGrammar or Outlines) to enforce constraints

Key flow in interpreter (line 808-809):
```python
for item in [
    # ...other params...
    "dtype",
    "regex", 
    "json_schema",
]:
    value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)
    if value is not None:
        setattr(clone, item, value)
```

### 5. **Backend Sampling Parameters**

Lower-level API defined in `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`:
```python
class SamplingParams:
    def __init__(self,
                 # ... standard params ...
                 json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
                 regex: Optional[str] = None,
                 ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
                 structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
                 # ... other params ...
    ):
```

**Constraint backends supported:**
- **XGrammar** (default) - Supports: JSON schema, regex, EBNF
- **Outlines** - Supports: JSON schema, regex
- **LLGuidance** - Supports: JSON schema, regex, EBNF

### 6. **Test Files for Constraint Testing**

Key test mixins in `/python/sglang/test/kits/`:
- **`json_constrained_kit.py`** - Tests JSON schema constraints
- **`regex_constrained_kit.py`** - Tests regex constraints (with 7+ regex patterns)
- **`ebnf_constrained_kit.py`** - Tests EBNF grammar constraints

Comprehensive test file:
- `/test/registered/constrained_decoding/test_constrained_decoding.py` - Combines all three constraint types

Test patterns (e.g., regex tests include):
- Email format: `r"^user@example\.com$"`
- Phone numbers: `r"^\(\d{3}\) \d{3}-\d{4}$"`
- Dates: `r"^2024-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-(0[1-9]|[12]\d|3[01])$"`
- Hex colors: `r"^#[0-9A-F]{6}$"`

### 7. **HTTP/REST API for Constraints**

Users can also specify constraints via direct HTTP requests to `/generate` endpoint:

```python
# JSON schema constraint
response = requests.post(
    "http://localhost:30000/generate",
    json={
        "text": "Introduce the capital of France...",
        "sampling_params": {
            "temperature": 0,
            "max_new_tokens": 128,
            "json_schema": json.dumps(schema),
        },
    },
)

# Regex constraint
response = requests.post(
    "http://localhost:30000/generate",
    json={
        "text": "Paris is the capital of",
        "sampling_params": {
            "temperature": 0,
            "max_new_tokens": 64,
            "regex": "(France|England)",
        },
    },
)

# EBNF constraint (XGrammar only)
response = requests.post(
    "http://localhost:30000/generate",
    json={
        "text": "Write a greeting.",
        "sampling_params": {
            "temperature": 0,
            "max_new_tokens": 64,
            "ebnf": 'root ::= "Hello" | "Hi" | "Hey"',
        },
    },
)
```

### 8. **Documentation Reference**

Main documentation:
- `/docs/basic_usage/sampling_params.md` - Complete parameter reference
- `/docs/advanced_features/structured_outputs.ipynb` - In-depth constraint usage guide
- `/docs/advanced_features/structured_outputs_for_reasoning_models.ipynb` - Reasoning model constraints

### 9. **Exported Symbols**

Constraints are NOT directly exported in `/python/sglang/__init__.py` - users access them through the **`gen()` function parameters** rather than separate constraint classes.

### 10. **Key Constraints Validation**

From `SamplingParams.verify()` method:
```python
grammars = [self.json_schema, self.regex, self.ebnf]
if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
    raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
```

Only **one** constraint type allowed per `gen()` call.

Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me compile a comprehensive report of the constraint implementation findings:

## Summary: SGLang Output Constraints Implementation

Based on my comprehensive search of the SGLang codebase, here's how output constraints (regex, JSON schema, and choice/select constraints) are implemented:

### 1. Constraint Types and Entry Points

**Files involved in constraint definitions:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775392221800-2e0e6a/python/sglang/lang/api.py` - Main API for `gen()`, `select()` and constraint parameters
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775392221800-2e0e6a/python/sglang/lang/ir.py` - Intermediate representation with `SglGen` and `SglSelect` classes
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775392221800-2e0e6a/python/sglang/lang/choices.py` - Choice sampling methods

#### The `gen()` function signature (api.py):
```python
def gen(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    min_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    n: Optional[int] = None,
    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
    min_p: Optional[float] = None,
    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
    dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]] = None,          # Type constraint
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,               # Choice constraint
    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
    regex: Optional[str] = None,                       # Regex constraint
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,                 # JSON schema constraint
)
```

#### The `select()` function:
```python
def select(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temperature: float = 0.0,
    choices_method: ChoicesSamplingMethod = token_length_normalized,
)
```

### 2. IR Classes (`ir.py`)

**SglGen class** - Stores generation parameters including constraints:
- Wraps constraints in `SglSamplingParams` dataclass which includes:
  - `json_schema: Optional[str]`
  - `regex: Optional[str]`
  - `dtype: Optional[str]`
  - `ebnf: Optional[str]`
  - `structural_tag: Optional[str]`

**SglSelect class** - Encodes discrete choices:
- `choices: List[str]`
- `temperature: float`
- `choices_method: ChoicesSamplingMethod`

### 3. Constraint Pipeline: From User Code to Execution

**Step 1: API Layer** (`lang/api.py`):
- `gen()` creates `SglGen(...)` with constraint parameters
- `select()` creates `SglSelect(...)`

**Step 2: Interpreter Layer** (`lang/interpreter.py`):
- `_execute_gen()` extracts sampling params and calls `backend.generate()`
- `_execute_select()` calls `backend.select()`

**Step 3: Backend Layer** (`lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py`):
- `RuntimeEndpoint.generate()` converts dtype to regex if needed
- Uses `sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs()` to pass constraints to runtime server

**Step 4: SamplingParams** (`srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`):
- `SamplingParams` class validates constraints:
  - Only ONE of `regex`, `json_schema`, or `ebnf` can be set
  - Stores: `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf`, `structural_tag`
- `to_srt_kwargs()` serializes to server format

**Step 5: GrammarManager** (`srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`):
- `process_req_with_grammar()` - Determines if request needs grammar
- Routes constraints to appropriate grammar backend:
  - `("json", schema_string)` for JSON schema
  - `("regex", pattern_string)` for regex
  - `("ebnf", ebnf_string)` for EBNF
  - `("structural_tag", tag)` for structural tags
- Uses thread pool to asynchronously compile constraints

### 4. Grammar Backends (`srt/constrained/`)

**Files:**
- `base_grammar_backend.py` - BaseGrammarBackend abstract class with dispatch methods
- `grammar_manager.py` - Manages constraint compilation and caching
- `xgrammar_backend.py` - XGrammar implementation (primary backend)
- `outlines_backend.py` - Outlines library integration
- `llguidance_backend.py` - LLGuidance integration

**Backend Registry:**
```python
create_grammar_backend(server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids)
# Selects backend based on server_args.grammar_backend:
# - "xgrammar" (default, most capable)
# - "outlines"
# - "llguidance"
# - "none" (no constraints supported)
```

### 5. Constraint Execution in Sampling

**SamplingBatchInfo** (`srt/sampling/sampling_batch_info.py`):
- Manages batched sampling constraints
- `update_regex_vocab_mask()` - Creates vocabulary masks from grammar
- `apply_logits_bias()` - Applies vocab masks to logits during sampling

**The constraint flow:**
1. Grammar object loaded/compiled from cache or compiled on-the-fly
2. For each generation step:
   - Grammar state advanced with accepted token
   - Vocab mask allocated and filled with valid next tokens
   - Mask applied to logits BEFORE sampling
   - Only valid tokens have non-zero logit values

### 6. Dtype Constraint Handling

**File:** `lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py`

Dtype constraints are converted to regex patterns:
```python
if sampling_params.dtype in ["int", int]:
    dtype_regex = REGEX_INT  # r"[-+]?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
    sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
elif sampling_params.dtype in ["float", float]:
    dtype_regex = REGEX_FLOAT  # r"[-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
    sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
elif sampling_params.dtype in ["str", str]:
    dtype_regex = REGEX_STR  # r"\"[\w\d\s]*\""
elif sampling_params.dtype in ["bool", bool]:
    dtype_regex = REGEX_BOOL  # r"(True|False)"
```

### 7. Choice/Select Constraint Handling

**File:** `lang/choices.py`

Implements different choice scoring methods:
- `TokenLengthNormalized` - Highest normalized prompt logprob (default)
- `GreedyTokenSelection` - Greedy token-by-token selection
- `UnconditionalLikelihoodNormalized` - Normalized by unconditional logprobs

Choices are evaluated by:
1. Running generation for each choice in parallel
2. Computing token-level log probabilities for each choice
3. Applying scoring method to select best choice

### 8. JSON Schema Constraint Utilities

**File:** `srt/function_call/utils.py`

Helper functions for tool/function calling with JSON schemas:
- `get_json_schema_constraint()` - Generates JSON schema for tool choices
- `_get_tool_schema_defs()` - Extracts and consolidates `$defs` from schemas
- `infer_type_from_json_schema()` - Infers parameter types from schemas

### 9. Request Storage and Passing

**File:** `srt/managers/schedule_batch.py`

The `Req` class stores:
```python
class Req:
    sampling_params: SamplingParams  # Contains regex, json_schema, ebnf
    grammar: Optional[BaseGrammarObject]  # Compiled grammar object
    grammar_key: Optional[Tuple[str, str]]  # ("json"|"regex"|"ebnf", constraint_string)
```

### 10. Benchmarks and Examples

**Usage examples:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775392221800-2e0e6a/benchmark/json_schema/bench_sglang.py` - JSON schema constraint example
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775392221800-2e0e6a/benchmark/json_decode_regex/` - Regex constraint examples
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775392221800-2e0e6a/test/registered/function_call/test_json_schema_constraint.py` - Comprehensive tests

### 11. Key Registration Points

Constraints are **NOT** explicitly registered in a registry in the traditional sense. Instead, they are:
1. **Defined at API layer** - `gen()` and `select()` functions
2. **Routed dynamically** - Based on which constraint parameter is non-None
3. **Backend-dispatched** - Grammar backends implement dispatch methods for each constraint type
4. **Cached** - Compiled grammars are cached using `(constraint_type, constraint_string)` tuples as keys

The registration happens implicitly through:
- `BaseGrammarBackend.dispatch_json()`, `.dispatch_regex()`, `.dispatch_ebnf()`, `.dispatch_structural_tag()`
- Implemented by concrete backends (XGrammar, Outlines, LLGuidance)

Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me compile a comprehensive report of the constraint implementation findings:

## Summary: SGLang Output Constraints Implementation

Based on my comprehensive search of the SGLang codebase, here's how output constraints (regex, JSON schema, and choice/select constraints) are implemented:

### 1. Constraint Types and Entry Points

**Files involved in constraint definitions:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775392221800-2e0e6a/python/sglang/lang/api.py` - Main API for `gen()`, `select()` and constraint parameters
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775392221800-2e0e6a/python/sglang/lang/ir.py` - Intermediate representation with `SglGen` and `SglSelect` classes
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775392221800-2e0e6a/python/sglang/lang/choices.py` - Choice sampling methods

#### The `gen()` function signature (api.py):
```python
def gen(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    min_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    n: Optional[int] = None,
    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
    min_p: Optional[float] = None,
    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
    dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]] = None,          # Type constraint
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,               # Choice constraint
    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
    regex: Optional[str] = None,                       # Regex constraint
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,                 # JSON schema constraint
)
```

#### The `select()` function:
```python
def select(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temperature: float = 0.0,
    choices_method: ChoicesSamplingMethod = token_length_normalized,
)
```

### 2. IR Classes (`ir.py`)

**SglGen class** - Stores generation parameters including constraints:
- Wraps constraints in `SglSamplingParams` dataclass which includes:
  - `json_schema: Optional[str]`
  - `regex: Optional[str]`
  - `dtype: Optional[str]`
  - `ebnf: Optional[str]`
  - `structural_tag: Optional[str]`

**SglSelect class** - Encodes discrete choices:
- `choices: List[str]`
- `temperature: float`
- `choices_method: ChoicesSamplingMethod`

### 3. Constraint Pipeline: From User Code to Execution

**Step 1: API Layer** (`lang/api.py`):
- `gen()` creates `SglGen(...)` with constraint parameters
- `select()` creates `SglSelect(...)`

**Step 2: Interpreter Layer** (`lang/interpreter.py`):
- `_execute_gen()` extracts sampling params and calls `backend.generate()`
- `_execute_select()` calls `backend.select()`

**Step 3: Backend Layer** (`lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py`):
- `RuntimeEndpoint.generate()` converts dtype to regex if needed
- Uses `sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs()` to pass constraints to runtime server

**Step 4: SamplingParams** (`srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`):
- `SamplingParams` class validates constraints:
  - Only ONE of `regex`, `json_schema`, or `ebnf` can be set
  - Stores: `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf`, `structural_tag`
- `to_srt_kwargs()` serializes to server format

**Step 5: GrammarManager** (`srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`):
- `process_req_with_grammar()` - Determines if request needs grammar
- Routes constraints to appropriate grammar backend:
  - `("json", schema_string)` for JSON schema
  - `("regex", pattern_string)` for regex
  - `("ebnf", ebnf_string)` for EBNF
  - `("structural_tag", tag)` for structural tags
- Uses thread pool to asynchronously compile constraints

### 4. Grammar Backends (`srt/constrained/`)

**Files:**
- `base_grammar_backend.py` - BaseGrammarBackend abstract class with dispatch methods
- `grammar_manager.py` - Manages constraint compilation and caching
- `xgrammar_backend.py` - XGrammar implementation (primary backend)
- `outlines_backend.py` - Outlines library integration
- `llguidance_backend.py` - LLGuidance integration

**Backend Registry:**
```python
create_grammar_backend(server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids)
# Selects backend based on server_args.grammar_backend:
# - "xgrammar" (default, most capable)
# - "outlines"
# - "llguidance"
# - "none" (no constraints supported)
```

### 5. Constraint Execution in Sampling

**SamplingBatchInfo** (`srt/sampling/sampling_batch_info.py`):
- Manages batched sampling constraints
- `update_regex_vocab_mask()` - Creates vocabulary masks from grammar
- `apply_logits_bias()` - Applies vocab masks to logits during sampling

**The constraint flow:**
1. Grammar object loaded/compiled from cache or compiled on-the-fly
2. For each generation step:
   - Grammar state advanced with accepted token
   - Vocab mask allocated and filled with valid next tokens
   - Mask applied to logits BEFORE sampling
   - Only valid tokens have non-zero logit values

### 6. Dtype Constraint Handling

**File:** `lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py`

Dtype constraints are converted to regex patterns:
```python
if sampling_params.dtype in ["int", int]:
    dtype_regex = REGEX_INT  # r"[-+]?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
    sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
elif sampling_params.dtype in ["float", float]:
    dtype_regex = REGEX_FLOAT  # r"[-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
    sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
elif sampling_params.dtype in ["str", str]:
    dtype_regex = REGEX_STR  # r"\"[\w\d\s]*\""
elif sampling_params.dtype in ["bool", bool]:
    dtype_regex = REGEX_BOOL  # r"(True|False)"
```

### 7. Choice/Select Constraint Handling

**File:** `lang/choices.py`

Implements different choice scoring methods:
- `TokenLengthNormalized` - Highest normalized prompt logprob (default)
- `GreedyTokenSelection` - Greedy token-by-token selection
- `UnconditionalLikelihoodNormalized` - Normalized by unconditional logprobs

Choices are evaluated by:
1. Running generation for each choice in parallel
2. Computing token-level log probabilities for each choice
3. Applying scoring method to select best choice

### 8. JSON Schema Constraint Utilities

**File:** `srt/function_call/utils.py`

Helper functions for tool/function calling with JSON schemas:
- `get_json_schema_constraint()` - Generates JSON schema for tool choices
- `_get_tool_schema_defs()` - Extracts and consolidates `$defs` from schemas
- `infer_type_from_json_schema()` - Infers parameter types from schemas

### 9. Request Storage and Passing

**File:** `srt/managers/schedule_batch.py`

The `Req` class stores:
```python
class Req:
    sampling_params: SamplingParams  # Contains regex, json_schema, ebnf
    grammar: Optional[BaseGrammarObject]  # Compiled grammar object
    grammar_key: Optional[Tuple[str, str]]  # ("json"|"regex"|"ebnf", constraint_string)
```

### 10. Benchmarks and Examples

**Usage examples:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775392221800-2e0e6a/benchmark/json_schema/bench_sglang.py` - JSON schema constraint example
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775392221800-2e0e6a/benchmark/json_decode_regex/` - Regex constraint examples
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775392221800-2e0e6a/test/registered/function_call/test_json_schema_constraint.py` - Comprehensive tests

### 11. Key Registration Points

Constraints are **NOT** explicitly registered in a registry in the traditional sense. Instead, they are:
1. **Defined at API layer** - `gen()` and `select()` functions
2. **Routed dynamically** - Based on which constraint parameter is non-None
3. **Backend-dispatched** - Grammar backends implement dispatch methods for each constraint type
4. **Cached** - Compiled grammars are cached using `(constraint_type, constraint_string)` tuples as keys

The registration happens implicitly through:
- `BaseGrammarBackend.dispatch_json()`, `.dispatch_regex()`, `.dispatch_ebnf()`, `.dispatch_structural_tag()`

3	import dataclasses

11	REGEX_INT = r"[-+]?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
12	REGEX_FLOAT = r"[-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
13	REGEX_BOOL = r"(True|False)"
14	REGEX_STR = r"\"[\w\d\s]*\""  # bugs with regex r"\".*\"" in interegular pkg

17	@dataclasses.dataclass
18	class SglSamplingParams:
19	    max_new_tokens: int = 128
20	    min_new_tokens: int = 0
21	    n: int = 1
22	    stop: Union[str, List[str]] = ()
23	    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = ()
24	    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = ()
25	    temperature: float = 1.0
26	    top_p: float = 1.0
27	    top_k: int = -1  # -1 means disable
28	    min_p: float = 0.0
29	    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
30	    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
31	    ignore_eos: bool = False
32	    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None
33	    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = (None,)
34	    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = (None,)
35	    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = (None,)
36	    json_schema: Optional[str] = None
37	
38	    # for constrained generation, not included in to_xxx_kwargs
39	    dtype: Optional[str] = None
40	    regex: Optional[str] = None
41	
42	    def clone(self):
43	        return SglSamplingParams(
44	            self.max_new_tokens,
45	            self.min_new_tokens,
46	            self.n,
47	            self.stop,
48	            self.stop_token_ids,
49	            self.stop_regex,
50	            self.temperature,
51	            self.top_p,
52	            self.top_k,
53	            self.min_p,
54	            self.frequency_penalty,
55	            self.presence_penalty,
56	            self.ignore_eos,
57	            self.return_logprob,
58	            self.logprob_start_len,
59	            self.top_logprobs_num,
60	            self.return_text_in_logprobs,
61	            self.json_schema,
62	        )
63	
64	    def to_openai_kwargs(self):
65	        # OpenAI does not support top_k, so we drop it here
66	        if self.regex is not None:
67	            warnings.warn("Regular expression is not supported in the OpenAI backend.")
68	        return {
69	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
70	            "max_completion_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
71	            "n": self.n,
72	            "stop": self.stop or None,
73	            "temperature": self.temperature,
74	            "top_p": self.top_p,
75	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
76	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
77	        }
78	
79	    def to_vertexai_kwargs(self):
80	        if self.regex is not None:
81	            warnings.warn(
82	                "Regular expression is not supported in the VertexAI backend."
83	            )
84	        return {
85	            "candidate_count": 1,
86	            "max_output_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
87	            "stop_sequences": self.stop,
88	            "temperature": self.temperature,
89	            "top_p": self.top_p,
90	            "top_k": self.top_k if self.top_k > 0 else None,
91	        }
92	
93	    def to_anthropic_kwargs(self):
94	        # Anthropic does not support frequency_penalty or presence_penalty, so we drop it here
95	        if self.regex is not None:
96	            warnings.warn(
97	                "Regular expression is not supported in the Anthropic backend."
98	            )
99	        return {
100	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
101	            "stop_sequences": (
102	                self.stop if isinstance(self.stop, (list, tuple)) else [self.stop]
103	            ),
104	            "temperature": self.temperature,
105	            "top_p": self.top_p,
106	            "top_k": self.top_k,
107	        }
108	
109	    def to_litellm_kwargs(self):
110	        if self.regex is not None:
111	            warnings.warn("Regular expression is not supported in the LiteLLM backend.")
112	        return {
113	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
114	            "stop": self.stop or None,
115	            "temperature": self.temperature,
116	            "top_p": self.top_p,
117	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
118	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
119	        }
120	
121	    def to_srt_kwargs(self):
122	        return {
123	            "max_new_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
124	            "min_new_tokens": self.min_new_tokens,
125	            "n": self.n,
126	            "stop": self.stop,
127	            "stop_token_ids": self.stop_token_ids,
128	            "stop_regex": self.stop_regex,
129	            "temperature": self.temperature,
130	            "top_p": self.top_p,
131	            "top_k": self.top_k,
132	            "min_p": self.min_p,
133	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
134	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
135	            "ignore_eos": self.ignore_eos,
136	            "regex": self.regex,
137	            "json_schema": self.json_schema,
138	        }

141	class SglFunction:
142	    def __init__(self, func, num_api_spec_tokens=None, bind_arguments=None):
143	        self.func = func
144	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
145	        self.bind_arguments = bind_arguments or {}
146	        self.pin_prefix_rid = None
147	
148	        # Parse arguments
149	        argspec = inspect.getfullargspec(func)
150	        assert argspec.args[0] == "s", 'The first argument must be "s"'
151	        self.arg_names = argspec.args[1:]
152	        self.arg_defaults = argspec.defaults if argspec.defaults is not None else []
153	
154	    def bind(self, **kwargs):
155	        assert all(key in self.arg_names for key in kwargs)
156	
157	        new_bind_dict = {**self.bind_arguments, **kwargs}
158	        return SglFunction(self.func, bind_arguments=new_bind_dict)
159	
160	    def run(
161	        self,
162	        *args,
163	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
164	        n: int = 1,
165	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
166	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
167	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
168	        temperature: float = 1.0,
169	        top_p: float = 1.0,
170	        top_k: int = -1,
171	        min_p: float = 0.0,
172	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
173	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
174	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
175	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
176	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
177	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
178	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
179	        stream: bool = False,
180	        backend=None,
181	        use_thread: bool = True,
182	        **kwargs,
183	    ):
184	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program
185	
186	        # avoid using [] as the default arg: https://nikos7am.com/posts/mutable-default-arguments/
187	        if stop is None:
188	            stop = []
189	        if stop_token_ids is None:
190	            stop_token_ids = []
191	        if stop_regex is None:
192	            stop_regex = []
193	
194	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
195	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
196	            n=n,
197	            stop=stop,
198	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
199	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
200	            temperature=temperature,
201	            top_p=top_p,
202	            top_k=top_k,
203	            min_p=min_p,
204	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
205	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
206	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
207	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
208	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
209	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
210	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
211	        )
212	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
213	        return run_program(
214	            self,
215	            backend,
216	            args,
217	            kwargs,
218	            default_sampling_para,
219	            stream,
220	            use_thread=use_thread,
221	        )
222	
223	    def run_batch(
224	        self,
225	        batch_kwargs,
226	        *,
227	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
228	        n: int = 1,
229	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
230	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
231	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
232	        temperature: float = 1.0,
233	        top_p: float = 1.0,
234	        top_k: int = -1,
235	        min_p: float = 0.0,
236	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
237	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
238	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
239	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
240	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
241	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
242	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
243	        backend=None,
244	        num_threads: Union[str, int] = "auto",
245	        progress_bar: bool = False,
246	        generator_style: bool = False,
247	    ):
248	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program_batch
249	
250	        if stop is None:
251	            stop = []
252	        if stop_token_ids is None:
253	            stop_token_ids = []
254	        if stop_regex is None:
255	            stop_regex = []
256	
257	        assert isinstance(batch_kwargs, (list, tuple))
258	        if len(batch_kwargs) == 0:
259	            return []
260	        if not isinstance(batch_kwargs[0], dict):
261	            num_programs = len(batch_kwargs)
262	            # change the list of argument values to dict of arg_name -> arg_value
263	            batch_kwargs = [
264	                {self.arg_names[i]: v for i, v in enumerate(arg_values)}
265	                for arg_values in batch_kwargs
266	                if isinstance(arg_values, (list, tuple))
267	                and len(self.arg_names) - len(self.arg_defaults)
268	                <= len(arg_values)
269	                <= len(self.arg_names)
270	            ]
271	            # Ensure to raise an exception if the number of arguments mismatch
272	            if len(batch_kwargs) != num_programs:
273	                raise Exception("Given arguments mismatch the SGL function signature")
274	
275	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
276	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
277	            n=n,
278	            stop=stop,
279	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
280	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
281	            temperature=temperature,
282	            top_p=top_p,
283	            top_k=top_k,
284	            min_p=min_p,
285	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
286	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
287	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
288	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
289	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
290	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
291	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
292	        )
293	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
294	        return run_program_batch(
295	            self,
296	            backend,
297	            batch_kwargs,
298	            default_sampling_para,
299	            num_threads,
300	            progress_bar,
301	            generator_style=generator_style,
302	        )
303	
304	    def trace(self, *, backend=None, **kwargs):
305	        from sglang.lang.tracer import trace_program
306	
307	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
308	        return trace_program(self, kwargs, backend)
309	
310	    def cache(self, backend=None):
311	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import cache_program
312	
313	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
314	        return cache_program(self, backend)
315	
316	    def __call__(self, *args, **kwargs):
317	        from sglang.lang.tracer import TracingScope
318	
319	        tracing_scope = TracingScope.get_current_scope()
320	        if tracing_scope is None:
321	            return self.run(*args, **kwargs)
322	        else:
323	            kwargs["backend"] = tracing_scope.tracer_state.backend
324	            return self.trace(*args, **kwargs)
325	
326	
327	class SglExpr:
328	    node_ct = 0
329	
330	    def __init__(self):
331	        self.node_id = SglExpr.node_ct
332	        self.prev_node = None
333	        self.pid = None
334	        SglExpr.node_ct += 1
335	
336	    def __add__(self, other):
337	        if isinstance(other, str):
338	            other = SglConstantText(other)
339	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr)
340	
341	        return self.concatenate_ir(self, other)
342	
343	    def __radd__(self, other):
344	        if isinstance(other, str):
345	            other = SglConstantText(other)
346	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr), f"{other}"
347	
348	        return self.concatenate_ir(other, self)
349	
350	    def concatenate_ir(self, a, b):
351	        if isinstance(a, SglExprList):
352	            if isinstance(b, SglExprList):
353	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + b.expr_list)
354	            else:
355	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + [b])
356	        elif isinstance(b, SglExprList):
357	            return SglExprList([a] + b.expr_list)
358	
359	        return SglExprList([a, b])
360	
361	    def print_graph_dfs(self):
362	        ret = [""]
363	        visited = set()
364	
365	        def dfs_print(x):
366	            if x is None or x in visited:
367	                return
368	            visited.add(x)
369	
370	            # Print dependency
371	            if x.prev_node is not None:
372	                dfs_print(x.prev_node)
373	
374	            if isinstance(x, SglExprList):
375	                for y in x.expr_list:
376	                    dfs_print(y)
377	            # elif isinstance(x, SglRole):
378	            #    dfs_print(x.expr)
379	            elif isinstance(x, SglVariable):
380	                dfs_print(x.source)
381	
382	            # Print the node itself
383	            if isinstance(x, (SglFork, SglGetForkItem)):
384	                ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = {x}\n"
385	            else:
386	                if x.prev_node is not None:
387	                    ret[0] += (
388	                        f"%{x.node_id} = %{x.prev_node.node_id} + " + str(x) + "\n"
389	                    )
390	                else:
391	                    ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = " + str(x) + "\n"
392	
393	        dfs_print(self)
394	        return ret[0]
395

632	
633	    def _process_expr(self, expr):
634	        if isinstance(expr, SglGen):
635	            self.name = self.process_name_for_reasoning(expr.name)
636	        elif isinstance(expr, SglSelect):
637	            self.name = self.process_name_for_reasoning(expr.name)
638	        elif isinstance(expr, SglExprList):
639	            for x in expr.expr_list:
640	                self._process_expr(x)
641	
642	    def __repr__(self):
643	        return f"SeparateReasoning(model_type={self.model_type}, name={self.name})"
